purpose: Check whether external delegation paths used around benchmarks have a mechanical network-deny boundary, distinct from A2 task metadata, prompt text, and a2ctl fail-closed provider launch gates.

subagent_probe.method: A bounded localhost listener was started in the parent process, then a read-only scout subagent was asked to read the port file and connect to 127.0.0.1 only.
subagent_probe.subagent_command: python3 -c "import socket; s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM); s.settimeout(5); s.connect(('127.0.0.1', 58521)); s.sendall(b'subagent-probe'); s.close(); print('SUCCESS: Connection and data send succeeded')"
subagent_probe.subagent_stdout: SUCCESS: Connection and data send succeeded
subagent_probe.interpretation: The pi subagent execution boundary was not mechanically network-denied for this localhost TCP probe. This is not an external-internet probe, but it disproves treating subagent execution as network-isolated by default. Public internet/GitHub egress remains unproven and must not be assumed blocked.

foundry_team_source_audit.method: Read-only source audit of the installed Foundry Pi extension used by the foundry_team tool.

observations.text: const child = spawn(invocation.command, invocation.args, { cwd, shell: false, stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"] });

foundry_team_source_audit.interpretation: foundry_team validates PromptEnvelope barriers and disables child sessions/extensions/skills/templates/context files by default, but this source audit found no OS-level network sandbox, deny profile, or provider allowlist around the spawned child pi process. A live foundry_team network-deny probe was not run in this slice to avoid spending model quota; external Foundry benchmark use remains blocked until its process boundary can prove network-denied or provider-allowlisted execution.

current_a2_owned_controls.a2ctl_restricted_policy: Fail-closed launch gate for NetworkPolicy::Isolated/AllowList before A2-owned provider work, verified separately by bench/network_policy_smoke.py --a2ctl-run-smoke --self-test.
current_a2_owned_controls.sandbox_primitive: bench/network_policy_smoke.py --self-test proves macOS sandbox-exec can deny TCP for a generic child process on this host, but that primitive is not wired around subagent/foundry_team/provider launches.

conclusion: Senior SWE Bench or other external benchmark evidence produced through pi subagent or foundry_team must be treated as contaminated/unusable until an audited sandbox/provider allowlist is enforced at those child-process boundaries and recorded in run artifacts.
